115580.explanation: We agree. A referendum gives residents a clear voice and increases involvement in local politics. Together, this ensures decisions have broader support.

115583.explanation: We agree. Farmers should have the space to supplement their business with, for example, a shop or a campsite. This helps them maintain a healthy income and contributes to a lively and vital rural area, as long as it fits well with the surroundings.

115586.explanation: We disagree. Our municipality has already made agreements through an exchange scheme. We contribute in a different way through that. It is important to adhere to these agreements and make choices that fit the capacity and possibilities of our municipality.

115587.explanation: We agree. Employers in agriculture and horticulture know the needs of their seasonal workers. By making them responsible for housing on their own premises, they can provide good, safe, and suitable living places close to the workplace. This is practical and fair for both parties.

115591.explanation: We don't know yet. A municipal fireworks display can bring fun and togetherness, but the nationwide fireworks ban raises questions about safety and regulations. We believe this should be carefully considered before a decision is made.

115592.explanation: We agree. Free bulky waste collection at home makes it easier for residents to dispose of waste safely and neatly. This keeps the municipality clean, prevents littering, and helps residents at no extra cost.

115593.explanation: We disagree. Festivals and parties bring fun and liveliness to the municipality. Stricter noise standards could limit this. With good agreements and customized measures, noise nuisance can be reduced without events losing their place.

115595.explanation: We don't know yet. Farmers could sometimes build their own homes on their farmyards, for example for family members or employees, but on agricultural land this often goes too far. It is important that customization is applied per situation, so it fits the environment and zoning plan.

115597.explanation: We agree. The local broadcaster connects residents and brings important information about our municipality close to home. Extra support helps maintain quality and continuity, so everyone stays informed about news and events in Heerde.

115600.explanation: We agree. Our sports clubs and volunteers form the heart of the community. If they experience difficulties, the municipality must financially support them so they can continue to exist and offer a place for sports, gatherings, and social connections in Heerde.

115603.explanation: We disagree. Safety is important, but in Heerde there is more need for police control and presence than for additional municipal enforcement officers (boa’s). This way problems can be addressed more effectively and professionally.

115604.explanation: We don't know yet. Camera surveillance can help with safety, but it is not necessary or desirable everywhere. Strategically deploying cameras where really needed offers a good balance between safety and privacy.

115607.explanation: We agree. By giving them priority for social rental housing, we support our residents and strengthen the local community.

115611.explanation: We agree. People receiving social assistance benefits can make a valuable contribution through meaningful social work. It is important that this is tailored per person, so it fits their abilities, talents, and personal situation.

115612.explanation: We don't know yet. A “low income” is not the same for everyone and can vary per situation. It is important that the municipality continues to pay attention to residents struggling with taxes and provides customized solutions where needed, so no one gets into financial trouble.

115613.explanation: We disagree. The income threshold for poverty assistance has already been raised recently. It is important to continue monitoring whether this threshold is sufficient and, if necessary, review and adjust it per situation.

115614.explanation: We disagree. Instead of cutting, it is better to increase resources so there remains enough attention and activities for the needs of young people.

115615.explanation: We disagree. This comes at the expense of too many regular parking spaces. It is better to find a balance and place charging points only where truly needed, so everyone has enough parking space.

115617.explanation: We disagree. It is important that people receive care they need, but due to the large shortage of caregivers, it is not always possible to guarantee a choice based on religion, culture, or belief. The focus is on accessible and quality care for everyone.

115619.explanation: We agree. Municipal taxes, such as property tax (ozb), are relatively high in Heerde.

115622.explanation: We agree. Expanding business parks offers employment and space for (local) entrepreneurs to grow. This strengthens the economy in Heerde and creates opportunities for residents and companies in the municipality.

115624.explanation: We disagree. Refugees with a residence permit must join the queue like other housing seekers. This way everyone, including Heerde residents, gets a fair chance at social rental housing.

115628.explanation: We agree. School swimming is important for children's safety and confidence. By allocating funds to this, the municipality ensures all students have the opportunity to learn to swim well.

115629.explanation: We agree. We are proud of our language! Here you can speak the local dialect. Veluws is an important part of our culture and identity. By paying attention to this language in all primary schools, children learn their regional language and the heritage remains alive for future generations.

115632.explanation: We disagree. High residential buildings (3 floors) do not fit the character of Heerde and can disturb the living environment and village appearance. Low and medium-height buildings better fit the atmosphere and scale of our municipality.

115633.explanation: We disagree. Banning the rental of single rooms is too strict. For some young people or those needing temporary housing, renting a room is a good solution. It is better to assess this per situation rather than imposing a total ban.

115634.explanation: We agree. A village center strengthens social cohesion and offers residents a place for meeting, activities, and facilities. Having a village center in every village keeps life in all parts of Heerde lively and engaged.

115635.explanation: We agree. More parking spaces in village centers make it easier for residents and visitors to park their cars. This supports local shops, hospitality, and improves accessibility and livability in Heerde's centers.

115636.explanation: We disagree. A fully car-free center can improve livability and safety, but removing parking spaces can be difficult for residents and visitors. A low-traffic approach with parking spaces retained and smart routes, e.g., turning around through traffic, can be a customized solution.

115638.explanation: We agree. Youth care is very important and there is much going on in the municipality. If there is a shortage, the municipality should supplement from general funds so all youth receive the care and support they need.

115580.explanation: We are in favor of maximum active citizen participation. The referendum instrument has many limitations, such as representativeness, and misinformation can be disruptive. The municipal council is the democratically elected body.

115583.explanation: We definitely support that, but it cannot always be everywhere due to the Environmental Act and competition with local retailers.

115586.explanation: We believe every municipality should contribute its part. The municipality of Heerde accepts an extra quota of Ukrainians through a mutual exchange, and the municipality of Zutphen in turn accepts 'our' asylum seekers. If that exchange falls away, we believe Heerde should also comply with the Distribution Act.

115587.explanation: We cannot do without seasonal workers; otherwise, the economy comes to a halt. They do work that Dutch people often no longer want to do. Seasonal workers have the right to decent housing, but neighborhoods often do not welcome it. Therefore, housing on the premises where the businesses are located is a great plan for everyone.

115591.explanation: Certainly, in Heerde and also in Wapenveld. The New Year's dive at Heerderstrand is already a nice event.

115592.explanation: We currently have a good facility to dump green waste free every Saturday. The polluter must pay for bulky waste and construction waste.

115595.explanation: We already have the 'red for red' scheme. You demolish something and as a farmer you get space to build homes in return. Within these frameworks, there are already possibilities on farmyards, which is sufficient.

115597.explanation: Local broadcasting is fine, but the more local, the better, says this local Village Party.

115600.explanation: The municipality has already established a new accommodation policy; all sports facilities are the responsibility of the municipality. Our election program also states that subsidies should be indexed annually.

115603.explanation: Safety on the streets is priority number 1. Especially for women and also for young people, a personal connection with the enforcement officers (BOA's) is the start of better enforcement policy.

115604.explanation: Camera surveillance works preventively. We support that.

115607.explanation: We are very much in favor, in any case a significant percentage of rental homes must be designated for local residents. Laws and regulations should be adjusted accordingly.

115611.explanation: But based on what fits the possibilities of those involved, although the principle itself is completely fine.

115612.explanation: This is existing policy and we actually advocate for more customization, moving away from standard rules and generously assessing each case on the basis of established principles.

115613.explanation: We have just recently lowered that income limit and advocate for more case-by-case customization.

115614.explanation: Young people are the future; there are currently too few safe and appropriate facilities for them. Skate parks in Wapenveld and Heerde! It also starts with good youth workers who go out onto the streets to make contacts, rather than constantly introducing a new face with each new youth worker.

115615.explanation: To prevent all those cables running across the sidewalks. We find stimulating electric cars important. But once a car is charged, it should also leave the spot promptly.

115617.explanation: Agreed, but it must not come at the expense of the quality of care.

115619.explanation: Continue the current policy which means: increase with inflation adjustment, otherwise the municipality will get into trouble itself.

115624.explanation: Treat everyone equally, which obviously includes refugees with a residence status.

115628.explanation: Instead of subsidizing school swimming, the municipality should subsidize families who cannot afford swimming lessons themselves. We also support a new fully-fledged swimming pool in Heerde in the future and want to save for it from the annual surpluses.

115629.explanation: Your own culture is important, but it should be presented in a somewhat humorous way, of course.

115633.explanation: There is also a shortage of rooms, which can sometimes be a first step for young people.

115634.explanation: They already have that. In Veessen, MFC de Noord should be upgraded. In Vorchten, we advocate for equal financial treatment of MFA de Verbinding compared to other village centers.

115635.explanation: Definitely necessary, but based on the STOP principle, so priority for pedestrians, then cyclists, then public transport, and only then cars. However, people must be able to park their cars at a reasonable distance from the centers.

115636.explanation: The center must be low-traffic so that elderly and disabled people can still access it. Through traffic should be largely prevented. That is why we submitted a plan to reverse the direction of one-way traffic from north to south and then turn at Bonenburgerlaan.

115638.explanation: This is a task that the national government has transferred to the municipality and adequate national funding should accompany it. So it must not be supplemented from municipal resources.

115580.explanation: Citizen polls, experiments with new forms of representation and other ways to involve citizens in decision-making should be supplementary to the mandate given to the municipal council by the residents through elections.

115583.explanation: The agricultural sector deserves perspective. A sustainable agricultural sector has a healthy economic foundation, is innovative, handles soil, resources, and energy carefully, and produces in balance with animals and the living environment. We wholeheartedly support short food chains (farm shops).

115586.explanation: Ukrainian refugees are welcome. We also take our responsibility in asylum seeker reception and follow the distribution law to ensure balanced distribution in the Netherlands, where each municipality takes responsibility.

115587.explanation: We see a clear responsibility here for employers in agriculture and horticulture.

115591.explanation: The municipality allows private initiatives to organize fireworks shows; we do not see the execution of this as a municipal task.

115592.explanation: We have a planet with a finite amount of resources. Therefore, we want to be careful with what we use. We want to prevent waste where possible. Where waste does occur, strict separation policy applies.

115593.explanation: Events in Heerde must be safe and healthy. They should not only be well accessible but also come with clear (subsidy) agreements about waste and sustainability, noise, public health including attention to alcohol and drugs.

115595.explanation: We support building houses where it is desirable and possible. The environmental vision is decisive. Rules like 'red-for-red' can be helpful and we are open to possibilities for farmers who are ending their business activities.

115597.explanation: Independent local and regional media are crucial for local news provision and local democracy. They inform residents, keep officials alert, and set societal issues on the agenda. We strive for an active and open attitude towards local media. Subsidies can sometimes be necessary.

115600.explanation: Amateur sports receive support through accessible and sustainable sports facilities.

115603.explanation: We support active enforcement policy and the deployment of BOA’s to combat nuisance and for enforcement tasks. The number of BOA's has recently been increased.

115604.explanation: Not in general. Temporary measures in specific situations must be possible.

115607.explanation: We support local priority policies focusing on community building and supporting vulnerable groups. Starters must be able to start their housing careers in Heerde. We want to distribute scarcity fairly and create space for home seekers from Heerde.

115611.explanation: We advocate for positive social assistance policy in which people are not seen as a cost but are supported to contribute to their neighborhood and municipality. Obligations: no. Encouragement: definitely!

115612.explanation: The municipality of Heerde focuses on offering debt restructuring loans to people with problematic debts. This way, debts are bought out and only one creditor remains. This creates peace and perspective.

115613.explanation: In our municipality, the limit is set at 130% of the social assistance benefit.

115614.explanation: The municipality of Heerde invests in youth work that connects with the world of young people, including digitally.

115615.explanation: We advocate for sufficient parking spaces and public charging stations.

115617.explanation: Where possible, consideration is given to beliefs and backgrounds. This requires vision, consistent policy and clear choices from the municipality to use funds efficiently.

115619.explanation: The municipality faces major financial challenges due to cuts from the national government. A decrease in property tax (ozb) is not an option.

115622.explanation: Our goal is to add about 10 hectares of business parks in the coming years, as agreed regionally. Furthermore, we focus on better utilization of existing business parks.

115624.explanation: We support local priority policies focusing on community building and support for vulnerable groups. Urgency should be based on necessity with equal treatment of local residents and status holders.

115628.explanation: We believe it is important that children learn to swim and that there are sufficient opportunities nearby to maintain swimming skills. This does not have to be through school swimming lessons, which schools are not keen on either. A targeted approach for the 7% of children without a swimming diploma is desirable.

115629.explanation: Existing lessons should also pay attention to the Veluwe and Heerde dialects. Pride in your 'mother tongue' should be encouraged. The municipality can stimulate and support primary schools but should never impose this.

115632.explanation: High-rise buildings that fit the village character of Heerde are acceptable. These buildings must be well integrated into the environment and provided with greenery, for example, green roofs.

115633.explanation: There is high demand for rental houses. Renting out single rooms instead of houses is not in the interest of Heerde's residents. However, we do encourage housing division and the possibility to create student rooms.

115634.explanation: The centers of our villages Heerde and Wapenveld should be pleasant, safe places for residents and visitors to shop, eat, and relax.

115635.explanation: We apply the STOP principle: first Walking, then Cycling, Public transport, and finally Private transport. We realize that parking cars in villages, especially near facilities, requires attention. We advocate for sufficient parking spaces and public charging stations.

115636.explanation: The compact center of Heerde will be largely car-free and can be closed off to motorized traffic during events.

115638.explanation: The past period has been marked by structural financial deficits (including in youth care). Through the VNG, K80, and other channels, we urge the national government to provide municipalities with adequate compensation for the tasks they must perform.

115580.explanation: Referendums are a controversial tool in which nuance is often lacking and can easily lead to a one-sided majority will instead of a well-considered weighing of arguments. We are much more in favor of citizens' assemblies whose advice is adopted.

115583.explanation: In situations where possible and allowed, we believe farmers should have space for such initiatives, but they must fit into the environment.

115586.explanation: Heerde must loyally fulfill its obligations under the distribution law.

115587.explanation: We believe employers themselves are responsible for adequate housing for seasonal workers.

115591.explanation: A fireworks show is a disproportionate burden on air quality and we see that good alternatives are available. We prefer alternatives such as light shows.

115592.explanation: To prevent bulky waste from being dumped in nature, arrangements should be made for people with a low income.

115593.explanation: Of course, it is important to ensure the livability of our municipality remains guaranteed, but we see that residents can solve this themselves very well. Therefore, stricter standards are not necessary.

115595.explanation: Given the current housing shortage, all creative solutions should be possible to reduce this problem, with a focus on maintaining the livability and rural character of our villages.

115597.explanation: We are positive about our local broadcaster, but we also have other challenges in our municipality. If extra funding is needed, we will decide on that later.

115600.explanation: Sports clubs play an important social role, but it is not realistic to maintain every sports club. Naturally, the municipality must do its utmost to support sports clubs with advice and assistance.

115603.explanation: Safety is very important to us, but we see no reason to deploy more enforcement officers (boa's) for this.

115604.explanation: We support targeted and proportional use of cameras, but preferably solve problems in other ways.

115607.explanation: For social reasons, we want to give residents priority in obtaining a certain portion of the social housing to be allocated.

115611.explanation: We support involving people in socially valuable work, but coercion is not the right way to make people feel valuable to our society.

115612.explanation: We believe that people with debts are helped by forgiveness of municipal taxes. The municipality must focus maximally on preventing people from falling into debt crises.

115613.explanation: We believe that a higher income limit prevents more people from falling into debt problems, thus ultimately reducing costs for the municipality.

115614.explanation: The deployment of youth work can help prevent youth-related disturbances. Preventive measures provide significant savings compared to corrective measures.

115615.explanation: We support electric driving, but adding extra parking spaces should only be done when truly necessary. The availability of charging points must be aligned with demand.

115617.explanation: We believe that free choice of care is important for an inclusive society.

115619.explanation: We do not support a generic reduction of the property tax (OZB).

115622.explanation: We believe there is still sufficient space in existing business parks and therefore see no need for expansion. We want to be attractive to green and sustainable companies and support regional cooperation for the development of business parks.

115624.explanation: We believe we must meet statutory obligations, and we find it important that people with a residence status have the opportunity to become part of our society.

115628.explanation: We consider it important for the safety of our children that they learn to swim. We are willing to allocate funds for this provided there is support from parents and schools.

115629.explanation: We believe it is important that our children learn to read, write, and calculate properly. These skills are under great pressure. In addition, we want to invest in our own Heerde identity. We think these goals can be combined.

115632.explanation: We think there are suitable places within the municipality for the construction of stacked (multi-story) housing. We see the advantage that this creates more space for green areas and playgrounds for children.

115633.explanation: The municipality may and must set conditions, but this solution can also contribute to reducing the housing shortage.

115634.explanation: We find it important for livability that villages have and maintain a clear village center.

115635.explanation: We are not automatically in favor of extra parking spaces. It is important to find a balance between accessibility, livability, and safety of our villages.

115636.explanation: We are a municipality with a high quality of life. This fits with a car-reduced center where everyone can walk safely and comfortably.

115638.explanation: We believe that cutting youth care budgets ultimately leads to higher costs in the future, so we want to invest in vulnerable young people to prevent them from getting into trouble. We support prevention and well-coordinated care where quality comes first.

115580.explanation: We must primarily listen to our residents, which is different from just hearing them! For important decisions, our residents must be able to express their voices and those voices must count.

115583.explanation: Farmers (agricultural entrepreneurs) are already being targeted enough. Farmers are under pressure. We want to enable other business models as much as possible. We do seek balance with the immediate environment.

115586.explanation: We are against an asylum seekers' center (AZC). After all, we are already accommodating extra Ukrainians.

115587.explanation: Of course, seasonal workers must be housed decently and humanely, but according to us, this does not necessarily have to be on their own land.

115591.explanation: We find the tradition of carbide shooting much more important. You want to celebrate New Year's in your own environment/neighborhood with your own friends.

115592.explanation: That is unaffordable. Then rates must be increased. Those who produce the waste must also pay the costs.

115593.explanation: There are already good rules. We do not enforce them sufficiently and must act more strictly.

115595.explanation: We must facilitate retiring farmers as well as possible where possible. We find it important that farmers themselves have the opportunity to develop those homes.

115597.explanation: Local broadcasters are legally funded by the national government. This has been adjusted in recent years. The municipality no longer has a role in this.

115600.explanation: Associations are responsible for keeping themselves afloat.

115603.explanation: We believe there should be more police on the streets, not more enforcement officers (BOAs).

115604.explanation: We see no need for more camera surveillance, unless there is a specific local reason. We must avoid creating a society where "Big Brother is watching you" becomes the norm.

115607.explanation: These people already have it hard enough. We think that people with an economic connection to the municipality of Heerde should also be given priority.

115611.explanation: We consider this a good starting point.

115612.explanation: By law, the municipality is not allowed to engage in income-based policies.

115613.explanation: Working must continue to pay off.

115615.explanation: This is a responsibility of the people themselves. As a municipality, we must ensure that solutions are found for people without their own land, for example by providing a cable duct in the sidewalk.

115617.explanation: The care demand should be leading. Furthermore, we support maintaining the separation of church and state in the Netherlands, including in government-funded care.

115619.explanation: The property tax (OZB) should be kept as low as possible.

115622.explanation: We must meet the expansion needs of our local businesses. Entrepreneurs keep our economy running so that we can continue to pay for our welfare state.

115624.explanation: The need is too great. We believe that not giving priority also sends an incentive to the national government to reduce immigration.

115628.explanation: The drowning of your child is terrible. Children must learn to swim. As far as we are concerned, the municipality supports school swimming.

115629.explanation: That is the responsibility of the parents.

115632.explanation: In certain locations, we should be able to allow up to four residential floors (maximum now is three) to create more housing, for example for starters.

115633.explanation: In our opinion, this mainly occurs in cities.

115634.explanation: But we already have that. Multi-functional centers (MFA) in Heerde and Wapenveld. MFC in Vorchten and De Noord in Veessen.

115635.explanation: We have enough parking spaces. We are not in favor of sacrificing water or greenery to create even more parking spots.

115636.explanation: We support a combined solution, but not always completely car-free. This should be determined in consultation with entrepreneurs.

115638.explanation: Youth care is very important. We believe youth care should first look at becoming more effective itself without increasing costs for the municipal budget.

115580.explanation: D66 for Heerde considers it important that residents think along early about important choices. Many subjects are complex and cannot be captured by a simple yes or no. That is why we focus on participation and consultation beforehand, rather than a referendum afterwards.

115583.explanation: D66 for Heerde wants to give farmers room to do business and diversify. This can be done with initiatives such as farm shops or small-scale recreation, as long as they fit the environment and do not cause nuisance or damage to nature and the living environment.

115586.explanation: D66 for Heerde takes responsibility for reception, but a full-fledged asylum seekers' center (azc) does not fit the scale of our municipality. Therefore, we choose regional cooperation and other forms of reception that better suit Heerde.

115587.explanation: D66 for Heerde believes seasonal workers have the right to good and safe housing. Employers have a responsibility here, but it does not necessarily have to be on their own company premises. What counts is quality, supervision, livability, with room for customization.

115591.explanation: D66 for Heerde believes that a municipal fireworks show is not a core task. The municipality can provide space for initiatives but does not have to organize it itself.

115592.explanation: D66 for Heerde believes the costs of bulky waste collection should be borne by the provider. Those who offer a lot of bulky waste pay more than those who have little.

115593.explanation: D66 for Heerde sees no reason to further tighten sound standards at festivals and parties. The current rules provide sufficient protection and room for local events.

115595.explanation: D66 for Heerde wants to handle agricultural land and landscape carefully. Additional housing on farms may be possible in specific situations, for example as compensation for retiring farmers, and not as a general rule.

115597.explanation: D66 for Heerde believes a strong, independent local broadcaster is important for a well-informed society. Targeted support can contribute to this.

115600.explanation: D66 for Heerde finds sports clubs indispensable for health, social contact, and unity. If clubs get into trouble, the municipality must provide financial support where necessary and possible, so that sport remains accessible to everyone.

115603.explanation: D66 for Heerde considers safety and livability important. Whether more enforcement officers are needed depends on the situation. We prefer smart deployment of BOAs, good cooperation, and prevention rather than just focusing on numbers.

115604.explanation: Camera surveillance can help in specific places, but D66 for Heerde opts for targeted use and careful consideration, not a general expansion.

115607.explanation: D66 for Heerde believes social rental homes should be allocated fairly and transparently. Local ties can sometimes be taken into account, but general priority for residents of the municipality itself is not appropriate.

115611.explanation: D66 for Heerde believes people on welfare need support and perspective. A mandatory obligation to give something back does not do justice to the often complex situations people are in.

115612.explanation: Remission can help prevent people with low incomes from getting into further trouble. D66 for Heerde wants to retain the possibility of remission.

115613.explanation: D66 for Heerde believes poverty policy must be effective and well-founded. Sometimes widening income limits can help, but only if it clearly supports people and prevents problems.

115614.explanation: D66 for Heerde sees youth work as an investment in our youth. It contributes to prevention, support, and timely detection of problems. That is why we consider cutting youth work unwise.

115615.explanation: D66 for Heerde wants to enable the transition to electric driving. For this, sufficient and well-distributed charging points are needed in the villages.

115617.explanation: D66 for Heerde believes care should match as well as possible with people's backgrounds and wishes. Where possible, this should be taken into account, but full freedom of choice is not always feasible.

115619.explanation: D66 for Heerde believes the property tax (OZB) should be affordable and understandable. Lowering it is not a goal in itself and should not come at the expense of facilities and investments in the municipality.

115622.explanation: D66 for Heerde provides space for businesses. Expansion of industrial areas is possible, provided it is done carefully and sustainably. The landscape and living environment remain leading in this.

115624.explanation: D66 for Heerde believes that refugees with a residence permit should be able to participate quickly. Housing is important in this regard. At the same time, we continue to ensure sufficient social rental homes for everyone, so that no one is set against each other.

115628.explanation: D66 for Heerde values swimming safety, but a broad implementation of school swimming is costly and unnecessary. We choose to provide targeted support for children who do not yet have a swimming diploma.

115629.explanation: D66 for Heerde finds it valuable if schools can pay attention to regional dialects and local culture, such as Veluws. The municipality can facilitate this.

115632.explanation: Building higher can help with the housing challenge, but it is not suitable everywhere. D66 for Heerde therefore does not exclude it. Suitable locations include the edges of expansion sites, provided this is well integrated into a green living environment and fits the scale of Heerde.

115633.explanation: D66 for Heerde does not consider a general ban on renting out rooms necessary. Renting rooms can contribute to the housing supply and suits different living arrangements. Where abuses occur, the municipality must take action, but a blanket ban is unnecessary.

115634.explanation: D66 for Heerde finds meeting places and facilities in villages important. How this is shaped differs per locality. Sometimes having a village center of its own is appropriate, sometimes existing or shared facilities work better. Customized solutions and efficient use of buildings are paramount.

115635.explanation: D66 for Heerde looks per locality at what is needed for accessibility and livability. More parking spaces are not a goal in itself.

115636.explanation: A (partly) car-free center can contribute to livability. D66 for Heerde does believe that entrepreneurs, residents, and suppliers must remain easily accessible.

115638.explanation: D66 for Heerde believes youth care should be available to those who need it. If that temporarily means the municipality has to step in, we will do so. At the same time, it remains unacceptable that the national government provides municipalities structurally insufficient funds for this.

statements.title: Heerde should cover any shortfall in youth care using the municipality's general funds.

titleParts.text: Heerde moet een tekort in de

titleParts.glossary: Support for young people with problems, for example mental health care, foster care, day programmes, and help with dyslexia.

titleParts.text: aanvullen vanuit het algemene geld van de gemeente.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The center of Heerde should become car-free.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Residents of Heerde should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Heerde moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Heerde should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: More camera surveillance should be installed in Heerde.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
High-rise apartment buildings should be permitted in Heerde.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include education about the Veluws (Veluwe dialect).
The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
Business parks should be allowed to expand in Heerde.
